narration: Every curve is hiding a slope. And that slope changes everywhere you look.
Take this curve, f of x equals x squared. Watch what happens when we track the slope at every point.
See that green line? That's the tangent line. It shows the exact steepness right where the dot is. Watch the slope update as the dot moves.
Right here at the bottom, the curve is flat. The slope is zero.
But over here, the curve is climbing fast. The slope is four. Same curve, totally different steepness.
So how do we find the exact slope at any point? That question has a name.
It's called the derivative. The derivative tells you the exact slope of a curve at a single point.

And derivatives are everywhere. Slope of a position curve? That's velocity. Doctors track how fast a tumor grows. Businesses find marginal cost. Physicists calculate acceleration from a velocity curve.
That's what a derivative is. Next video, we'll show you exactly how to compute one.
